prerequisites: A Google account with access to Google Search Console.
At least one verified Search Console property whose URL you supply in the site_urls config field.
An account with Owner or Full User permission on the Search Console property (required to authorize OAuth access).
A Google Cloud project with the Search Console API enabled (required for self-managed deployments using your own credentials).
Replication window values: start_date (defaults to 2021-01-01) and optionally end_date, both formatted YYYY-MM-DD.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Authorize with an OAuth 2.0 client by supplying authorization.client_id, authorization.client_secret, authorization.refresh_token, and authorization.access_token. The authorizing account must have Owner or Full User permission on the Search Console property. Google requires OAuth 2.0 with the webmasters or webmasters.readonly scope; no other authorization protocol is supported for private data.

authMethods.name: Service Account Key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Google service account by supplying its JSON key in authorization.service_account_info. Create the service account in Google Cloud Console, grant it access, and for domain-wide delegation enable delegation and authorize the https://www.googleapis.com/auth/webmasters.readonly scope so it can read the Search Console property.

setupSteps.title: Verify your Search Console property
setupSteps.body: In Google Search Console, confirm you have at least one verified property. Record each property URL to enter later in the site_urls field. Ensure the account you will authorize with holds Owner or Full User permission on that property.

setupSteps.title: Enable the Search Console API in Google Cloud
setupSteps.body: In Google Cloud Console, select or create a project and enable the Search Console API. This is required when you provide your own OAuth client or service account rather than relying on a hosted OAuth app.

setupSteps.title: Create credentials (OAuth or Service Account)
setupSteps.body: For OAuth 2.0, create an OAuth client and obtain authorization.client_id, authorization.client_secret, authorization.refresh_token, and authorization.access_token using the webmasters or webmasters.readonly scope. For a service account, create one in Google Cloud Console, download its JSON key for authorization.service_account_info, and for domain-wide delegation grant the https://www.googleapis.com/auth/webmasters.readonly scope. Note the service account JSON key cannot be re-downloaded.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the chosen credentials under the authorization field and set site_urls to the verified property URLs you want to replicate. Optionally set start_date and end_date (YYYY-MM-DD), data_state, custom_reports, requests_per_minute, num_workers, and always_use_aggregation_type_auto to tune the sync.
